text.headline: Euro 96
text.text: Gareth Southgate (now England manager) mourns his missed penalty just before Moller slots home to seal the victory for Germany. England are defeated at the semi-finals - the furthest they had been in the competition since losing to Yugoslavia at the same round in 1968.

text.headline: France 98
text.text: David Beckham is sent off in their round of 16 game against Argentina at the World Cup. At 2-2, the game eventually goes to penalties. England are once again knocked out after missing twice from the spot. David Beckham is later sent death threats and bullets in the post.

text.headline: Euro 2000
text.text: England are roundly criticised for failing to qualify after a last minute penalty by Romania in the dying moments of the group stage game. The failure is not widely remembered compared to the drama of the 1998 World Cup.

text.headline: South Korea / Japan 2002
text.text: Ronaldinho celebrates after lobbing England goalkeeper David Seaman with an exceptional long-range free kick, sending England out of the World Cup at the quarter finals. Brazil eventually go on to win the competition.

text.headline: Euro 2004
text.text: Goalkeeper Ricardo saves without gloves as England lose again on penalties 6-5 against Portugal in the quarter finals. In a surprise upset the Portugese will stumble at the last hurdle to lose in the finals against a well-organised Greek side.

text.headline: Germany 2006
text.text: Wayne Rooney is given a straight red card after stamping on Ricardo Carvalho in England's quarter final match against Portugal. Ronaldo (far left) is seen shortly afterwards giving the Portugese bench an infamous wink. England go on to lose to Portgual again on penalties by 3-1.

text.headline: Euro 2008
text.text: England fail to qualify for Euro 2008, leading veteran football correspondent Henry Winter to write at the time: 'Outclassed, outfought and outthought'. Steve McClaren (pictured) was sacked in the immediate aftermath.

text.headline: South Africa 2010
text.text: England midfielder Frank Lampard reacts in disbelief as his goal is disallowed by the referee. The strike, fired at the underside of the crossbar is clearly seen to cross the line, and would have levelled the score against Germany 2-2. Germany go on to win 4-1 in the round of 16 match, their widest margin against England at a World Cup.

text.headline: Euro 2012
text.text: Italy are jubilant as England are defeated once again on penalties at the quarter final stage. Spain would later defeat Italy in the final, securing their place as the first team to win three major competitions in a row - Euro 2008, the 2010 World Cup in South Africa and Euro 2012.

text.headline: Brazil 2014
text.text: Luis Suarez of Uruguay heads home to secure his side's 2-1 victory over England. It is the first time England had been knocked out in the group stages of a World Cup since 1958.

text.headline: Euro 2016
text.text: England's defence is in disarray as an Icelandic player attempts a spectacular shot on goal. The round of 16 match ends 2-1 to Iceland after a lacklustre England performance. Legendary former England striker and broadcaster Gary Linekar described the result on Twitter as: 'the worst defeat in our history'.
